summary: The beta oxidation process for an odd chain fatty acid generates one acetyl-CoA and one propionyl-CoA as end products due to specific metabolic pathways involved at each step during degradation. This results from unique handling by enzymes compared to standard β-oxidation cycles seen in even carbon chains leading directly into multiple rounds without such intermediate steps producing two equal parts per cycle like typical cases do not occur here exactly twice but once plus another distinct product type uniquely formed only when dealing specifically with these types of molecules ending
